title: Within-lap ranking sidecar over `(Year,Race,LapNumber)` groups
summary: Pit calls are inherently relative: the question is often which nearby car pits first, not only whether a single row crosses a tyre threshold.
rationale: Pit calls are inherently relative: the question is often which nearby car pits first, not only whether a single row crosses a tyre threshold. Ranking losses are designed for grouped ordering and may capture within-lap pit priority better than plain binary logloss.
implementation_hint: Train a sidecar CatBoost `YetiRankPairwise`/`PairLogitPairwise` or LightGBM `lambdarank` model with query groups such as `(Year,Race,LapNumber)` and optionally restrict pairs to cars within a position/gap neighborhood. Feed its OOF score or rank percentile into the main binary model rather than replacing the classifier.
expected_effect: Should improve discrimination among ambiguous mid-window rows and sharpen who-pits-now ordering without requiring a full sequence model.
risk: Query groups can be noisy because many laps have zero or one positive; pairwise CatBoost also changes categorical handling and can be slower.
